RT @LabanMbunya: SEMA KIMEUMANA!

The United Opposition and Linda Mwananchi seem happy to create and fuel animosity at every turn by endorsing Edwin Sifuna. 

Rigathi Gachagua appears to have set aside his own presidential ambitions to back Fred Matiang'i. 

Meanwhile Kalonzo and Wamalwa have been pushed aside, though perhaps not permanently.

The public disagreement between George Natembeya and Eugene Wamalwa paints a bad picture for the opposition but hands the government a big advantage. 

Natembeya is ready to mount a full force campaign alongside the energetic Linda Mwananchi movement.

Eugene Wamalwa clearly feels sidelined in the current arrangement, which seems to favour only Fred Matiang'i, Edwin Sifuna and Kalonzo Musyoka as the main presidential contenders.

Justin Muturi also looks to have fallen out with Rigathi Gachagua. He is increasingly becoming ungovernable within this United Opposition setup and could soon show them the real birds of Kang'ethe.

The ticket might end up with Edwin Sifuna versus Ruto!

𝐎𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐜𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐬 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐟𝐢𝐚. 𝐈𝐧 𝐊𝐞𝐧𝐲𝐚, 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐟𝐢𝐚 𝐡𝐚𝐯𝐞 𝐚 𝐜𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐭𝐫𝐲.

In October 2013, a man called Bogonko Bosire disappeared.  Bogonko, previously a writer with Agence France-Presse, became a blogger for hire after losing his job. He ran a website, Jackal News, where it is said he would post articles for a round of beer. One of the posts he was hired to publish was a leak of the names of witnesses lined up to testify against then President Uhuru Kenyatta and his deputy, William Ruto, at the International Criminal Court (ICC), for their alleged roles in the 2007-2008 post-election violence.
 
Not long after - probably because of the incriminating knowledge he possessed - Bogonko mysteriously disappeared from the face of the earth. He was abducted, murdered, and his body was never found. Three months later, in December the same year, Albert Muriuki went missing from State House, Nairobi.  Muriuki had worked as an intern at the International Criminal Court (ICC) before being appointed to State House. Bogonko and Muriuki were “disappeared” after finishing their dirty job of publicly exposing ICC witnesses.
 
Many of the witnesses recanted their testimonies after being threatened, while others were paid off. A killer police squad called Kwekwe was formed to hunt down and kill the stubborn ones who refused to recant.  The ICC suspects knew who to kill or target because they had a hit list The Kwekwe squad officers were also eliminated after killing the witnesses. The ICC suspects hired a “cleaner” to eliminate the men they had used to do their dirty job.

While Bogonko and Muriuki likely got what they deserved for sleeping with the devil, there are four women who dared to resist the rich and powerful and did not deserve to be murdered.
 
Nancy Waithera, an employee of the National Hospital Insurance Fund (NHIF), was taken out by a sniper as she walked along Kaunda Street in Nairobi on February 17, 2023. She was a whistleblower in a Ksh 1.1 billion scandal involving NHIF CEO Geoffrey Mwangi, who had initially been suspended but returned after he was cleared by the court. Her killers are still walking scot-free.
 
On July 15, 2021, 67-year-old environmental activist Joannah Stutchbury was shot six times outside her Kiambu home as she got out of her car to clear the driveway, which had been blocked by tree branches.  At the time, the Director of Criminal Investigations (DCI), George Kinoti, said preliminary investigations indicated that Stutchbury might have been killed for her resistance to the encroachment and illegal construction of a road, and the grabbing of Kiambu forest.
 
Earlier in March of the same year, the body of a senior officer with the National Land Commission (NLC), Jennifer Itumbi Wambua, was discovered in a field in Ngong after she had been reported missing on March 12. Wambua was a key witness in a case where a deputy governor and a former top civil servant were charged with conspiring to defraud the government of Ksh 122.3 million.
Recently, another mysterious disappearance has hit the headlines. Esther Wairimu is the most senior legal officer at Kenya Forest Service HQ on Kiambu Road. She has served in the position for over 10 years.
On Tuesday, June 9, 2026, she reportedly arrived home from work and told her family that she was feeling uncomfortable after being asked to sign certain documents in the office. She vowed not to return to work.

Nevertheless, she reported to work at approximately 10 a.m. the following day and worked until around 2 p.m. when she left for her home in Juja. Upon reaching Juja town, she alighted from the vehicle she was travelling in and went into Senate Hotel to check out the gym, and disappeared.
For the past three weeks, search efforts by her family, friends, and colleagues have yielded no results. They combed the area around Juja on foot, by vehicle, and even with drones, but to no avail. She disappeared at a time when there are many attempts to grab public land, including inside the forest where she worked, Karura Forest. Yesterday, her decomposing body was found in a forest in Gatundu. She had been abducted and murdered.

In July 2022, two Indian nationals, Mohamed Zaid Sami Kidwai and Zulfiqar Ahmed Khan, alongside their Kenyan taxi driver Nicodemus Mwania Mwange were shot and killed by the police. When Ruto became president, he ordered the police officers charged with the killing to be prosecuted. Officers from the Special Service Unit who had abducted the three men, and those from the National Intelligence Service who had helped do the tracking, were arrested. Additionally, Kenya Wildlife Service officers who had helped dispose of the remains where they would be eaten by wild animals, were also charged. So, to the men and women carrying out abductions and assassinations on orders from above, you will face your day in court.

STATEMENT BY THE DCP DEPUTY PARTY LEADER SENATOR CLEOPHAS MALALA ON ETHNIC PROFILING IN KENYA BY UDA GOVERNMENT. 

Dear Kenyans,

1. Over the last couple of months, we have witnessed a well-structured ethnic profiling of the members of the kikuyu community by president William Ruto. This is a well-orchestrated move ahead of the 2027 elections in what Mr. William Ruto scripted in 2007 of the 41 vs 1. We all know what that led Kenya into. We lost over 1,300 Kenyans and hundreds of thousands of Kenyans, are up to date in IDP Camps.

2. The team leader for this narrative is one Mr. Kipchumba Murkomen seconded by Mr. Omar Hassan, the UDA Secretary General and several top government officials including cabinet secretaries whom we shall name here today.

3. The Minister for Interior should be advised better, and as a lawyer, much logic is expected of him. Reducing critical security issues into an ethnic clash between a kikuyu and Kalenjin affair is bad for our Nation Mr. Murkomen. As you do your simple mind games, cattle rustling in Meru is wreaking havoc. Mandera occupied by Jubaland forces, Goons are taking control of towns.

4. Leave politics and concentrate on your job Mr. Murkomen. Secure Kenyans. As a cabinet minister for interior, it is worrying to see you on a tribal charade trying to run EPRA and the energy docket on fuel prices when Kenyans are simply on their own.

5. Secondly, Mr. Omar, the Secretary General of the wheelbarrow party, we want to remind you of your role in the 2007/08 post-election violence ICC related witness coaching against Mr. William Ruto and others. Might you have forgotten, when you served as a commissioner at the Kenya National Human Rights Commissioner, Mr. William Ruto accused you od coaching witnesses against Mr. Ruto. In his own words, Ruto said, "I also want to inform Mr. Hassan Omar, who is the schemer, and the mercenary behind this whole scheme that that he cannot hire or rent, or buy or sell or negotiate justice and the truth...".

6. Mr. Omar, your house of lies, as described then by William Ruto, seems to have become a shopping mall. Stop profiling Kenyans of kikuyu origin. If you think it is the people of Kikuyu descent who have a problem with this government, when the truth dawns on you and your team, it is be too late to salvage yourselves. But unfortunately, you have already messed up the country.

7. When Kenyans come out in the streets to demonstrate about high costs of fuel, mutilated payslip, dysfunctional SHA, failed education systems, it is not Kikuyu Vs Kalenjin Community Mr. Kipchumba Murkomen. It is about 55 million Kenyans suffering high taxation, double standards, bad roads, poor policy among others.

8. When Kenyans in Northern Kenya are being butchered by the Jubaland Forces, it is not Kikuyu vs Kalenjin; it is Kenyans crying because the government has failed to protect them.

9. Teachers, civil servants, and all employees and Kenyans saying NO to Mr. William Ruto are doing so because of the personal pain they are going through courtesy of one Mr. William Ruto and not the Kalenjin community.

10. If, then, we buy your opaque arguments that 55 Million Kenyans are fighting Ruto because he is a Kalenjin, we then need to argue that William Ruto is the only Kalenjin in Kenya and the 55 million of us are all Kikuyus. So, Mr. Omar Hassan, please spare us your thoughts on negative ethnicity.

11. On the high cost of fuel, it is unfortunate that Kenyans are being treated to conmanship and blatant open thuggery through the pump.

As if the damage you have caused Kenya is not enough, you now want to mean it is because Ruto is a Kalenjin.

12. The government cannot purport that the high fuel cost only affects the matatu sector. Every Kenyan is affected by this insensitive fuel hike. But the good thing is, the government is digging its own political grave deeper. While addressing fuel costs, address every Kenyan, not a small pocket of transport sector.

For the past three weeks, search efforts by her family, friends, and colleagues have yielded no results. They combed the area around Juja on foot, by vehicle, and even with drones, but to no avail. 

By Boniface Mwangi

Your Claude Code setup doesn’t need to search docs by memory.

Knowledge RAG is a local RAG MCP server for Claude Code and other MCP clients that turns your PDFs, markdown, code, notebooks, and office docs into a searchable knowledge base.

It helps you query your own material without sending it to an external server by indexing files locally, combining BM25 keyword search with semantic vectors, and reranking results with a cross-encoder.

Key features:

• Local-first RAG – runs via ONNX with no API keys and no data leaving your machine
• Hybrid retrieval – combines semantic search, BM25, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and cross-encoder reranking
• Broad file parsing – supports 20 formats including PDF, Markdown, Python, JSON, CSV, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and notebooks
• MCP toolset – exposes 13 tools for search, document retrieval, reindexing, listing, URL ingestion, similarity, and evaluation
• Flexible setup – install through npx, pip, one-line scripts, Docker, or source, with optional NVIDIA GPU acceleration

It’s open-source (MIT license).

🔑Specialized Terminology Continues - 211/CCXI. Sir Humphry Davy’s Miners’ Safety Lamp - Vol. II, p. 353 - Davy’s lamp used fine wire gauze to prevent flame from passing outward and igniting explosive mine gases. 

The metal mesh absorbed and dispersed heat below the ignition threshold. It transformed delicate wire into an invisible thermal barrier.

212/CCXII. Wire Gauze as a Boundary against Fire
The safety lamp demonstrates that a boundary need not be solid to be effective. Openings permit light and air, yet the metallic network restrains flame. It is a profound example of selective passage, materially resembling a veil that transmits one influence while arresting another.
213/CCXIII. Davy’s Experiments on Copper Sheathing - Vol. III, p. 163
Davy investigated the protection of copper ship sheathing by attaching more reactive metals. His work applied electrochemical principles to corrosion. The protectors could preserve copper chemically yet interfere with its resistance to marine growth, revealing the complexity of engineering trade-offs.
214/CCXIV. Galvanic Protection and Sacrificial Metal
A less noble metal may corrode in preference to a more valuable structure. This sacrificial relationship offers a striking material correspondence: one body is consumed so that another may remain intact. In engineering it is cathodic protection; in symbolic reading it evokes preservation through substitution.
215/CCXV. Mr. Deeble’s Cast-Metal Blocks for Marine Works - Vol. I, p. 67
Deeble patented cast-metal blocks for caissons, jetties, piers, quays, and similar structures. His proposal extended foundry work into hydraulic civil engineering. Metal was imagined not merely as fastening but as monumental masonry capable of resisting tides, impact, and erosion.
216/CCXVI. De Lafons’s Remontoire Escapement - Vol. III, p. 308
De Lafons’s remontoire employed an intermediate mechanism that periodically stored and released a more uniform force to the escapement. It isolated the regulator from variations in the main spring or weight. The design is an early study in mechanical purification, separating irregular power from precise timekeeping.
217/CCXVII. Daniel Demetrius and Early Wire Manufacture - Vol. II, p. 330
Daniel Demetrius appears within the history of wire drawing and its introduction or development in Europe. The entry reflects the movement of specialist craftspeople across borders and the transfer of closely guarded mechanical knowledge.
218/CCXVIII. Dr. Dering’s Reliquiae Eboracenses - Vol. I, p. 20
Dr. Dering’s Latin poem or antiquarian work, Reliquiae Eboracenses, preserved notices concerning Yorkshire, ancient iron, local remains, or historical manufacture. It demonstrates how poetry and antiquarian Latin could serve as repositories of industrial archaeology.
219/CCXIX. Descostils, French Chemist - Vol. I, p. 34
Hippolyte-Victor Collet-Descotils was an eminent French chemist associated with mineral analysis and metallic substances. His inclusion marks the transition from visual ore classification to analytical chemistry, where hidden constituents were identified through reactions, precipitation, heat, and measurement.
220/CCXX. Detonating Priming - Vol. II, p. 119
Detonating priming used chemically sensitive compounds to ignite firearms by impact. It replaced older dependence upon exposed match or flint sparks. The technology joined early chemistry, percussion mechanics, salts, explosive decomposition, and compact ignition systems.
221/CCXXI. Fulminating Chemistry and Captive Fire
Percussion compounds store chemical instability within a minute cap. A small blow releases sudden flame. They represent fire held in latent molecular arrangement, awaiting mechanical command. Their usefulness came with extraordinary danger during manufacture and handling.
222/CCXXII. Dial Weighing Machine - Vol. II, p. 298
A dial weighing machine translated force or displacement into the circular movement of a pointer. The hidden action of springs and levers became visible as a reading upon a graduated face. It is an instrument of mechanical interpretation.
223/CCXXIII. Dignity of Ancient Smiths - Vol. I
Ancient smiths often held exceptional social or mythic standing because they controlled fire, weapons, tools, ornaments, and repair. Their knowledge could determine agricultural productivity, military survival, and royal splendour. The smith was feared, honoured, and sometimes placed near the sacred.
224/CCXXIV. Smith as Liminal Figure
The smith moves between natural and artificial states. He enters darkness for ore, kindles transformative fire, and brings forth shining form. This liminality helps explain why smiths were surrounded by myth, secrecy, taboo, divine patronage, and supernatural lore across many cultures.
225/CCXXV. Diodorus Siculus - Vol. I, p. 13
Diodorus preserved historical and ethnographic accounts concerning ancient peoples, mines, metals, weapons, and extraordinary customs. His writings became an important secondary witness for reconstructing ancient technology where material evidence and direct technical texts were scarce.
226/CCXXVI. Divining Rod - Vol. III, p. 11
The divining rod was used by some miners to search for water or metallic lodes. Its efficacy was disputed, yet its persistence reveals an older epistemology combining bodily sensation, inherited practice, landscape observation, mineral signs, and belief in hidden sympathies.
227/CCXXVII. Dowsing and Subtile Correspondence
In early natural magic, the rod could be interpreted through sympathy, attraction, effluvia, or invisible rays between matter and sensitive operator. Modern geology does not require such supernatural causation, yet the practice remains historically important as evidence of how miners understood concealed nature before geophysics.
228/CCXXVIII. Donkin and Bacon’s Rotary Printing Machine - Vol. II, p. 232
Donkin and Bacon’s machine used rotary principles to accelerate printing. Cylinders, gearing, inking, feeding, and pressure converted repeated impressions into a coordinated mechanical cycle. The machine made industrial motion serve textual multiplication.
229/CCXXIX. Drawing Bench - Vol. III, p. 245
The drawing bench pulled brass tubes or metal through dies to reduce diameter, improve surface, and control wall thickness. It converted tensile force into dimensional refinement. Drawing creates precision by compelling material through a fixed geometric threshold.
230/CCXXX. Dryburgh Bridges - Vol. I, p. 106
The Dryburgh suspension bridges belonged to early experiments in chain-supported crossings. Their history illustrates the testing of iron tension systems before later monumental suspension bridges. Such structures render invisible tensile force visible through sagging curves and anchored chains.
231/CCXXXI. Guillaume de Salluste Du Bartas on Smelting - Vol. I, p. 3
The French poet Du Bartas described the origin or idea of smelting ores in his cosmological poetry. His inclusion is especially important because it unites biblical creation, natural history, poetic imagination, mining, and metallurgy.
232/CCXXXII. Du Bartas and the Poetics of Molten Creation
Du Bartas imagines human beings learning from nature’s fires, perhaps volcanic or accidental, and discovering metal within flowing ore. Poetry becomes a hermeneutic bridge between creation and craft. The first furnace is interpreted as an imitation of processes already displayed in the earth.
233/CCXXXIII. M. Dufond’s Red-Hot Cast-Iron Sawing Experiments - Vol. II, p. 153
Dufond experimented with sawing cast iron while it remained red-hot. Heat alters hardness, cutting resistance, brittleness, and the behaviour of both tool and workpiece. The experiment belongs to thermal machining and the science of material state-dependent workability.
234/CCXXXIV. Cutting by Governing Material State
A substance difficult to cut when cold may become manageable at another temperature. The lesson extends throughout metallurgy: the correct operation depends upon encountering matter in the proper state. Timing is therefore a technical form of wisdom.
235/CCXXXV. Albrecht Dürer’s Steel Engravings - Vol. I, p. 275
Albrecht Dürer produced or was associated with early engraving upon iron or steel surfaces for printing. His work links Renaissance art, line, metallurgy, pressure, and reproducibility. Engraving turns resistance into precision, cutting images into a surface hard enough to preserve them.
236/CCXXXVI. Engraving as Inscriptional Ontology
The engraver removes minute portions of metal so that absence may carry ink. The image is born from disciplined incision. Meaning inhabits channels cut beneath the surface, making engraving a material hermeneutic of depth.
237/CCXXXVII. Dyer’s Patent Press for Perkins’s Plates - Vol. II, p. 238
Dyer’s press was designed for printing from hardened steel plates associated with Jacob Perkins’s secure engraving methods. The apparatus required immense, even pressure and accurate movement. It belongs to banknote security, anti-counterfeiting technology, steel engraving, and the industrial reproduction of intricate images.
238/CCXXXVIII. Perkinsian Security and Metallic Memory
Steel plates could produce far more impressions than copper and preserve fine lines over long runs. Their durability allowed complex patterns to guard currency and documents. Hardness became an instrument of trust.

239/CCXXXIX. Dynandrie - Vol. III, p. 155
“Dynandrie” appears as a rare term connected with the coppersmith or brazier. It derives from language concerning power, force, or paired operation and may describe a class of hammering, workshop arrangement, or cooperative labour. Because the term is obscure, it deserves preservation as part of the extinct vocabulary of metal manufacture.

240/CCXL. Dynandric Metallurgy as Cooperative Force
Where two workers coordinate hammering, holding, heating, or turning, craft becomes a distributed bodily machine. One worker reads the metal and directs; another supplies force. Their rhythm forms a temporary organism of hands, tools, heat, sound, and shared intention.

───

Interpretive Coda to C-D
The C-D portion of the index reveals several deep correspondences that ordinary alphabetical listings conceal:
• Cannon, bells, and gongs show metal acquiring thunder, warning, music, and public voice.
• Coins, type, engraving plates, and printing presses show metal acquiring language, authority, image, and memory.
• Clocks and chronometers show metal interpreting invisible time.
• Chains, locks, and anchors show metal governing attachment, boundary, security, and restraint.
• Copper precipitation, brass cementation, steel alloying, and gilding preserve the operative heart of alchemy within early chemistry.
• Meteoric iron, the Cyclopes, divining rods, and the dignity of smiths preserve ancient attempts to understand metallurgy through celestial descent, subterranean mystery, sympathy, myth, and sacred craftsmanship.
• Chimneys, cupolas, coke, calcination, and cannon reveal fire becoming architecturally governed.
• Microscopes, telescopes, and engraved images reveal matter becoming an instrument by which creation reads itself.
The alphabet therefore conceals a metallic cosmology. Under C and D alone, metal becomes bridge, boundary, body, voice, writing, weapon, clock, currency, image, memory, and instrument of discovery. The index is not merely a locator of pages. It is a compressed map of the mineral kingdom’s transformation into civilization.
